meta.name: Bay Area Terpene Locations Database
meta.description: Comprehensive location layer for the Vibe Curator platform. Maps Bay Area forests, parks, and botanical garden zones to their dominant terpene emissions, receptor targets, and cannabis strain matches. Covers SF, Marin, and Sonoma launch geography.

location_types.forest: Natural forest environment for full forest bathing immersion. Extended terpene exposure (1-3 hours). Highest phytoncide concentrations.
location_types.botanical_garden_zone: Curated micro-zone within SF Botanical Garden. Concentrated terpene exposure (5-15 minutes per zone). Ideal for targeted pre-loading walks.
location_types.urban_forest: Urban forest canopy within city limits. Moderate terpene exposure. Accessible without car or transit.

microclimate.description: Canyon on western slope of Mt. Tamalpais. Pacific fog creates wet environment year-round. Dense canopy = maximum terpene trapping.

terpene_profile.source_notes: Coast redwoods produce terpenoids and tannic acid in leaves, roots, bark, and wood. Dense old-growth canopy creates highest phytoncide concentration per cubic foot. Douglas fir understory adds myrcene. Cathedral Grove and Bohemian Grove are densest terpene zones.

experience_profile.best_time: Early morning or late afternoon for fog and fewer crowds
experience_profile.sensory_notes: Cathedral-like silence. Damp earth and pine. Crepuscular light rays through fog. Redwood Creek sounds.
key_zones: Cathedral Grove - densest canopy
Bohemian Grove - tallest trees
Fern Creek Trail - mixed species

cannabis_strain_matches.terpene_logic: Pinene-forward strains. Forest pre-loads α-pinene receptor systems.

access.notes: Free roadside parking on Nicasio Valley Road. New ADA-accessible boardwalk trails completed 2024.

microclimate.humidity: moderate - drier than other Marin redwood groves
microclimate.description: San Geronimo Valley. Drier microclimate than coastal groves - produces different understory plant community. No redwood sorrel (unlike other groves). Larsen Creek watershed runs through preserve.

terpene_profile.source_notes: Drier grove = lower overall phytoncide concentration but more concentrated pinene ratio. One of only four old-growth redwood areas in Marin. Different understory terpene mix due to absence of moisture-dependent species.

experience_profile.best_time: Morning for solitude. Any season works - each brings different character.
experience_profile.sensory_notes: Intimate and quiet. Fairy Ring redwood circles. New boardwalk trails create immersive elevated walk through canopy understory. Low crowds.
key_zones: Fairy Ring Trail - circular old-growth formation
Boardwalk Trail - immersive elevated path
Forest Trail - soft surface through canopy

cannabis_strain_matches.terpene_logic: Clean pinene profile without strong secondary terpenes. Good for pure pinene pre-loading.

locations.name: Samuel P. Taylor State Park
locations.address: 8889 Sir Francis Drake Blvd, Lagunitas, CA 94938

access.dogs_allowed: On Cross Marin Trail and fire roads only
access.notes: Camping among redwoods enables multi-day forest immersion. Pioneer Tree Trail (1.6 mi) is family-friendly.

locations.forest_type: Mixed coast redwood, oak woodland, and grassland

microclimate.description: Unique contrast of redwood canyon groves and open grassland. Lagunitas Creek / Papermill Creek creates riparian corridor. Multi-species forest = complex terpene cocktail in air.

terpene_profile.source_notes: MOST COMPLEX terpene profile of any location. Redwoods contribute pinenes, California bay laurel (Umbellularia californica) contributes 1,8-cineole and umbellulone, Douglas fir adds myrcene and β-pinene, madrone and tanoak add additional monoterpenes. Creek microclimate amplifies volatile emissions. Camping enables Li's 2-3 day immersion protocol (NK cell effects lasting 30+ days).

experience_profile.best_time: Summer for shaded respite. Winter for salmon runs and lush understory.
experience_profile.sensory_notes: Creek sounds throughout. Shifting terpene zones as you move between redwood canyon and open grassland. Bay laurel produces strong aromatic presence. Coho salmon spawn Dec-Jan.
key_zones: Pioneer Tree Trail - family-friendly old growth
Barnabe Peak Trail - elevation gradient terpene shift
Creek corridor - maximum humidity and volatiles

cannabis_strain_matches.terpene_logic: Complex multi-terpene profile. Pre-loads multiple receptor systems simultaneously. Best for full-spectrum entourage strains.

access.notes: Closest major redwood reserve to Solful Sebastopol. Adjacent Austin Creek Recreation Area has camping.

microclimate.description: Temperate rainforest. 55 inches annual rainfall. Abundant fog maintains moist conditions year-round. Fife Creek alluvial flat = maximum tree size. Peak phytoncide emission environment.

terpene_profile.source_notes: ANCHOR LOCATION for Solful Sebastopol. 1,400-year-old trees in temperate rainforest = peak phytoncide concentration. Highest annual rainfall of any location (55 inches). Alluvial flat setting maximizes tree growth and terpene production. Bay laurel and Douglas fir add secondary terpene complexity. Colonel Armstrong Tree and Parson Jones Tree are named specimens.

experience_profile.best_time: Early morning for fog and solitude. Spring for wildflower understory.
experience_profile.sensory_notes: Cathedral-like. 310-foot Parson Jones tree creates overwhelming scale. Temperate rainforest humidity amplifies scent. Redwood Forest Theater outdoor amphitheater. Winter rain turns understory into green carpet.
key_zones: Colonel Armstrong Tree - 1400 years old, 0.5 mi from entrance
Parson Jones Tree - tallest at 310 ft, 100 yards from entrance
Pioneer Nature Trail - wheelchair accessible
East Ridge Trail - elevation for different terpene exposure

cannabis_strain_matches.terpene_logic: Maximum pinene saturation environment. Ancient trees = highest terpenoid concentrations. Best for pinene-dominant strains consumed same evening.

access.notes: Free. Hidden gem near Occidental on Bohemian Highway wine route. Short loop trail. 15°F cooler inside canopy.

microclimate.description: Dramatic temperature drop inside canopy (15°F cooler). Creates condensed terpene atmosphere. Near coast between Sebastopol and Pacific. Forever Wild conservation easement.

terpene_profile.source_notes: Temperature drop inside grove condenses volatile terpenes into concentrated atmosphere. Anne's Circle is most dramatic section - dense old-growth in tight formation. Short trail but intense sensory experience. Near Bohemian Highway wine route for multi-sensory day.

experience_profile.best_time: Hot days for maximum temperature contrast. Mornings for solitude.
experience_profile.sensory_notes: Short but intense. Dramatic temperature shift at entrance like walking into natural AC. Anne's Circle is the most dramatic dense old-growth section. Picnic table available. Very few visitors.
key_zones: Anne's Circle - densest old-growth section
Main loop trail

cannabis_strain_matches.terpene_logic: Concentrated pinene atmosphere due to temperature inversion. Short intense exposure ideal for quick pre-loading before evening session.

locations.name: Mount Sutro Forest (Open Space Reserve)
locations.address: Mount Sutro Open Space Reserve, San Francisco, CA

access.notes: Hidden urban forest. Trail entrances tucked between houses. 80-acre eucalyptus cloud forest in heart of SF.

microclimate.fog_frequency: very high - within SF fog belt
microclimate.temperature_moderation: 10% cooler daytime, 5% warmer nighttime vs. outside
microclimate.fog_drip_increase: 300% more moisture from fog inside forest vs. outside
microclimate.description: Cloud forest microclimate. Fog belt location means eucalyptus trees precipitate fog year-round. 300% increase in fog-drip moisture inside forest. Air is saturated with eucalyptol vapors. 909 ft elevation - SF's third tallest hill.

terpene_profile.concentration_data: Eucalyptus globulus essential oil: 63-86% 1,8-cineole, 7-11% α-pinene, 3-7% limonene, 3-8% p-cymene
terpene_profile.source_notes: 80% eucalyptus forest creates dominant 1,8-cineole atmosphere. Fog-drip mechanism intensifies volatile release. UC Berkeley study showed eucalyptus groves moderate temperature and dramatically increase humidity, creating optimal conditions for terpene volatilization. Over 60 bird species. Poison oak present - stay on trails.

experience_profile.best_time: Foggy days for peak cloud forest atmosphere. Weekdays for solitude.
experience_profile.sensory_notes: On foggy days, may be most beautiful place in SF. Mist wraps treetops. Self-contained rain from fog drip. Ethereal, otherworldly atmosphere. 100-200 ft trees overhead. No city views from inside (unlike Twin Peaks). Feels like being transported entirely out of SF.
key_zones: Fairy Gates Trail - best entrance experience
North Ridge Trail - favorite path, winding through eucalyptus and coast live oaks
Rotary Meadow - summit, California native plants surrounded by eucalyptus

cannabis_strain_matches.terpene_logic: Eucalyptol-dominant atmosphere. Pre-loads AChE inhibition and anti-inflammatory pathways. Best for strains with cooling/menthol notes.

locations.name: Presidio Ecology Trail
locations.address: Ecology Trail, Presidio, San Francisco, CA

access.notes: 1.4-mile trail. Upper trail pedestrian only. Multi-biome = most diverse terpene exposure per step in Bay Area.

microclimate.description: Multi-biome trail crosses through redwood grove, eucalyptus forest, serpentine grassland, and mixed conifer forest in 1.4 miles. Each zone has distinct terpene atmosphere.

terpene_profile.source_notes: MOST DIVERSE terpene exposure per distance walked. Eucalyptus zones produce 1,8-cineole. Redwood grove produces pinenes. Monterey pine/cypress produce different pinene ratios and sabinene. Serpentine grassland produces aromatic sage compounds. You cross through 4-5 distinct terpene zones in a single short hike. Inspiration Point overlook at south end.

experience_profile.best_time: Any time. Sunset for views from Inspiration Point.
experience_profile.sensory_notes: Shifting aromatic zones as you walk. Eucalyptus scent gives way to pine gives way to redwood gives way to grassland. Bay views from Inspiration Point. Art installations by Goldsworthy add contemplative dimension.
key_zones: Redwood grove - pinene zone
Eucalyptus forest near Wood Line - cineole zone
Serpentine grassland - aromatic herb zone
Inspiration Point - panoramic views

cannabis_strain_matches.terpene_logic: Multi-terpene pre-loading across diverse zones. Activates multiple receptor systems in sequence. Best for complex terpene profile strains.

locations.name: Mount Tamalpais State Park

access.notes: Steep Ravine has old-growth redwoods. Summit has chaparral. TCC Trail accesses redwoods without Muir Woods reservation.

locations.forest_type: Elevation gradient: coast redwood → mixed hardwood → chaparral

microclimate.description: Dramatic elevation gradient from sea level to 2,571 ft. Each elevation band has distinct vegetation and terpene profile. Lower canyons = redwood/humid. Mid-slopes = mixed hardwood. Summit = aromatic chaparral with sage and manzanita. Above fog line at summit.

terpene_profile.source_notes: Elevation gradient creates shifting terpene zones. Steep Ravine old-growth redwoods produce pinenes. Mid-slope bay laurel produces 1,8-cineole and umbellulone. Summit chaparral (California sage, manzanita) produces camphor, thujone, linalool. One of few places to experience three distinct terpene ecosystems in a single hike.

experience_profile.best_time: Clear days for summit views. Foggy days for lower trail atmosphere.
experience_profile.sensory_notes: Dramatic sensory shifts as you climb. Damp redwood canyon → dry chaparral scrub. Fog below at summit. Panoramic views of SF Bay, Pacific, and Marin. Can see Solful SF's neighborhood from summit.
key_zones: Steep Ravine - old-growth redwoods, highest pinene
TCC Trail - redwoods without Muir Woods reservation
Summit - aromatic chaparral, sage and manzanita
Pantoll trailhead - access to multiple zones

cannabis_strain_matches.terpene_logic: Multi-zone pre-loading. Lower = pinene priming. Summit = camphor/linalool priming. Choose elevation zone based on target strain profile.

locations.name: Angel Island State Park

access.notes: Ferry ride creates sensory transition from city. Island microclimate traps volatiles. 5-mile Perimeter Road loop.

locations.forest_type: Mixed eucalyptus groves, bay laurel woodland, coastal scrub

microclimate.description: Island microclimate traps terpenes. Bay breezes circulate volatiles. Mixed native (bay laurel, coast live oak) and planted (eucalyptus) groves. Southwest slopes have Sunset Trail through shady bay laurel forest.

terpene_profile.source_notes: Dual 1,8-cineole sources - eucalyptus groves and native bay laurel produce same primary terpene from different botanical origins. Perfect demonstration of terpene source independence thesis. Island microclimate traps volatiles. Sunset Trail through bay laurel/oak forest is most aromatic section.

experience_profile.best_time: Clear days for bay views. Spring for wildflowers.
experience_profile.sensory_notes: Ferry crossing creates ritual transition from urban to natural. Salt air → eucalyptus → bay laurel → open grassland as you hike. Immigration Station history adds contemplative weight. 360° bay views from Mt. Livermore summit.
key_zones: Sunset Trail - bay laurel and coast live oak, most aromatic
Perimeter Road - through eucalyptus groves
Mt. Livermore summit - open grassland and coastal scrub

cannabis_strain_matches.terpene_logic: 1,8-cineole dominant from dual botanical sources. Demonstrates source independence. Pre-loads respiratory and cognitive pathways.

locations.name: Pomo Canyon Trail
locations.address: Pomo Canyon Trail, Sonoma Coast, CA

access.notes: Coastal proximity + redwood canopy = fog-trapped terpene chamber. Near Russian River cannabis corridor. Fairy ring redwood formations.

microclimate.description: Redwood canyon near Sonoma Coast. Coastal fog + redwood canopy creates natural terpene chamber. Fairy ring formations (redwoods growing in circles) concentrate phytoncides in enclosed spaces.

terpene_profile.source_notes: Fairy ring formations create natural enclosed terpene chambers - redwoods growing in circles trap air and concentrate phytoncides. Coastal fog keeps humidity high and enhances volatile release. Near Pomo Canyon Campground.

experience_profile.best_time: Summer mornings when fog is thick in canyon.
experience_profile.sensory_notes: Standing inside a fairy ring of redwoods is a uniquely enclosed terpene experience. Coastal mist drifts through canyon. Ferns and wildflowers in understory.
key_zones: Fairy ring formations near lower campsites
Canyon bottom - maximum terpene concentration

cannabis_strain_matches.terpene_logic: Concentrated pinene in natural enclosures. Fairy rings are nature's diffuser chambers.

locations.name: San Francisco Botanical Garden

access.entry_fee: Free before 9am daily, free for SF residents, free 2nd Tuesday monthly. $13 non-resident.
access.notes: 4-MINUTE WALK from Solful SF (900 Irving St). Main entrance at 9th Ave & Lincoln Way. North/Friend Gate on MLK Jr Drive.

locations.proximity_note: Closest dispensary-to-terpene-garden pairing in America. Customer can walk from Solful to living terpene laboratory in under 5 minutes.

zones.name: Garden of Fragrance
zones.location_within_garden: Just inside main gate, turn right
zones.description: Designed in 1965 for visually impaired. Raised beds for touch-and-smell. 12th-century Spanish monastery stones (shipped by William Randolph Hearst). Bronze Saint Francis sculpture. Winding pathways and garden benches.

terpene_profile.source_notes: Contains botanical sources of nearly half of your 18 essential oils in living form. Raised beds designed for releasing scent through touch. Multi-receptor activation zone hitting GABA-A (linalool), CB2 (β-caryophyllene from patchouli), and cholinergic systems (1,8-cineole from rosemary) simultaneously. Peak bloom in summer.

experience_profile.best_time: Summer for peak lavender, salvia, and pelargonium bloom.
experience_profile.sensory_notes: Touch plants to release volatiles. Layer scents by moving between beds. Designed as sensory experience, not just visual. Sun-dappled. Intimate scale.

cannabis_strain_matches.terpene_logic: Linalool + cineole pre-loading. Primes anxiolytic and cognitive pathways.

zones.name: Arthur L. Menzies Garden of California Native Plants
zones.location_within_garden: South side, 4 acres. Access from main entrance heading south.
zones.description: 4-acre California native plant garden designed by Ron Lutsko Jr. in 1986. Arroyos, ponds, woodlands, and wildflower meadow. 70+ manzanita species. ASLA Honor Award for Design. Being redesigned with new interpretive signs in 2026.

terpene_profile.source_notes: California sages produce exact terpenes driving anxiolytic effects in Silexan research (Article 2). 70+ manzanita species provide α-pinene + myrcene diversity. Coyote mint is wild menthol/pulegone source. Native California terpene pharmacy in curated form.

receptor_targets: GABA-A (linalool from sage, pinene from manzanita)

experience_profile.best_time: April-May for peak wildflower bloom. Summer for sage aromatics.
experience_profile.sensory_notes: Aromatic sages dominate when brushed. Manzanita bark has distinctive smooth red texture. Wildflower meadow in spring. Bird and insect activity. Feels wild despite being in the city.

cannabis_strain_matches.terpene_logic: Sage-dominant terpene exposure. Primes GABA-A and cholinergic systems. Best for strains with herbal/sage nose.

zones.name: Redwood Grove (SF Botanical Garden)
zones.location_within_garden: South-central. Access from North/Friend Gate or from Menzies Garden.
zones.description: Century-old coast redwoods (planted ~1900). Only redwood forest within San Francisco city limits. Includes rare albino redwood at section 48F. Multiple sub-sections (48A through 48R). Succulent Garden on adjacent hillside.

terpene_profile.source_notes: Same species and terpene emission as Muir Woods, in urban setting. Century-old trees have had time to establish mature terpene production. Fog-drip from Golden Gate Park maritime influence keeps grove humid. Albino redwood demonstrates genetic variation in same species.

experience_profile.best_time: Foggy summer mornings. Hot days for cool shade.
experience_profile.sensory_notes: Feels remarkably like entering Muir Woods but you're in San Francisco. Temperature drops noticeably. Can combine with adjacent Succulent Garden for sensory contrast.

cannabis_strain_matches.terpene_logic: Urban pinene pre-loading. Same molecules as Muir Woods, 4 minutes from the dispensary.

zones.name: Conifer Lawn
zones.location_within_garden: Western section, between New Zealand collection and Redwood Grove
zones.description: 30+ conifer species around large lawn. Part of garden's 250+ total conifer species collection. Includes giant sequoia (Sequoiadendron giganteum), fir (Abies) and spruce (Picea) species. Enormous Monterey cypress in center. Nobel Dwarf Conifer Garden, Dawn Redwood Grove also nearby.

terpene_profile.source_notes: BROADEST PINENE DIVERSITY in one location. Each of 30+ species produces different α-pinene/β-pinene ratios plus unique secondary terpenes (δ-3-carene from fir, camphene from spruce, sabinene from cypress). Walking through this area is like sampling 30+ variations of pinene-dominant essential oils. Giant sequoia is only one in SF.

experience_profile.best_time: Any time - conifers are evergreen year-round.
experience_profile.sensory_notes: Open lawn setting allows you to move between individual specimen trees and compare their aromatic profiles. Different pine/fir/spruce species have subtly different scents - this is terpene terroir in action.

cannabis_strain_matches.terpene_logic: Pinene diversity - 30+ variations of the same primary terpene. Ideal for understanding how minor ratio differences create distinct experiences.

zones.name: Mediterranean Garden
zones.description: Plants from all five Mediterranean climate regions: California, Mediterranean Basin, Chile, South Africa, and Southwestern Australia. Culinary herbs, aromatic shrubs, Echium species.

terpene_profile.source_notes: Five Mediterranean climate zones concentrated. Culinary herbs = familiar terpene sources. Overlapping 1,8-cineole, thymol, carvacrol, and linalool profiles. Strong aromatic presence especially in warm weather when volatiles increase.

experience_profile.best_time: Warm sunny days when volatile emission peaks.
experience_profile.sensory_notes: Familiar culinary aromatics in garden setting. Warm sun releases oils from herb leaves. Echium flowers add visual spectacle. Can brush herbs to intensify scent release.

cannabis_strain_matches.terpene_logic: Multi-herb terpene blend. Cineole + linalool + thymol combo. Pre-loads multiple receptor systems for complex strains.

zones.name: Australian Collection
zones.description: Plants from wet and dry regions of Australia. Eucalyptus species, Banksia, tea tree relatives, spear lilies (Doryanthes palmeri). Part of original canopy (Eucalyptus globulus planted 1800s).

terpene_profile.source_notes: Eucalyptus globulus was one of three original canopy species (450,000 trees planted in mid-1800s). Australian section concentrates multiple eucalyptus species plus tea tree relatives (Melaleuca family produces terpinen-4-ol). Living demonstration of eucalyptol production across related but distinct species.

experience_profile.best_time: Any time - eucalyptus are evergreen. Warm days increase volatile emission.
experience_profile.sensory_notes: Eucalyptus scent is immediately recognizable. Banksia have bold, otherworldly shapes. Tea tree relatives add medicinal aromatic notes.

cannabis_strain_matches.terpene_logic: Eucalyptol saturation zone. Same primary terpene as Mount Sutro but in curated garden setting.

zones.name: Monterey Cypress & Pine Canopy
zones.location_within_garden: Throughout garden, especially Great Meadow and perimeter
zones.description: Iconic Monterey Cypress silhouettes frame the entire garden skyline. Part of original 450,000-tree canopy planting (mid-1800s). Monterey Pine (Pinus radiata) throughout. These create persistent background terpene atmosphere for entire 55-acre site.

terpene_profile.source_notes: Background terpene layer for entire garden. These canopy trees create ambient pinene atmosphere from moment of entry. Monterey cypress and pine produce distinct pinene ratios from redwoods - different α/β pinene balance and unique sabinene component. You're getting passive terpene pre-loading throughout your entire visit regardless of which zone you're in.

experience_profile.best_time: Always present. Windy days distribute volatiles more broadly.
experience_profile.sensory_notes: You may not consciously notice this canopy terpene layer, but it's pharmacologically active. The iconic Monterey Cypress silhouettes against the skyline are the visual marker. Great Meadow is the best open-air location to sense the ambient canopy.

cannabis_strain_matches.terpene_logic: Ambient background pre-loading. Enhances all other zone exposures.

solful_sf_quick_preload.name: The 45-Minute Terpene Walk (Solful SF → Botanical Garden → Solful SF)
solful_sf_quick_preload.description: Walk from Solful, enter Botanical Garden at main gate (free before 9am), hit 3 terpene zones, return to Solful with receptor systems primed.

solful_sf_full_immersion.name: The Full Spectrum Walk (All 7 Botanical Garden Zones)
solful_sf_full_immersion.description: Complete terpene circuit through all seven zones. Maximum receptor diversity pre-loading.

solful_sf_plus_sutro.name: The Eucalyptol Deep Dive (Solful SF → Botanical Garden → Mt. Sutro)
solful_sf_plus_sutro.description: Australian Collection at Botanical Garden then Mt. Sutro cloud forest. Maximum 1,8-cineole exposure.

solful_sebastopol_redwood_circuit.name: The Sonoma Pinene Circuit (Solful Sebastopol → Grove of Old Trees → Armstrong)
solful_sebastopol_redwood_circuit.description: Two old-growth redwood experiences near Sebastopol. Maximum pinene pre-loading.

marin_forest_bathing_full.name: The Marin Terpene Trail (Full Day)
marin_forest_bathing_full.description: Multiple Marin forests for maximum phytoncide diversity and duration.

terpene_timing_notes.nocturnal_emission_increase: 3.7x higher terpene emission rates at night vs. day (research finding)
terpene_timing_notes.fog_amplification: 300% increase in moisture inside eucalyptus forest vs. outside, intensifying volatile release
terpene_timing_notes.seasonal_peak: September has highest terpene diversity (α-pinene, myrcene, D-limonene, camphene, p-cymene all detected). January lowest.
terpene_timing_notes.temperature_effect: Warm sunny days increase volatile emission from herb gardens. Cool foggy conditions optimize conifer/eucalyptus emission.
terpene_timing_notes.optimal_protocol: Evening forest walks (sunset hikes) produce nearly 4x terpene exposure vs. midday. Ideal timing: late afternoon walk → evening cannabis session.
terpene_timing_notes.multi_day_immersion: Li et al. showed 2-3 day forest bathing trips produce NK cell effects lasting 30+ days. Samuel P. Taylor and Armstrong both offer camping for extended immersion.
